title: Check Point Threat Prevention Extraction Blocks
spl:
index=security sourcetype="cp:threatprevention" action=prevent
| stats count by protection_type, severity
| sort -count
description: Threat prevention blocks show inline protection value across blades.
value: Threat prevention blocks show inline protection value across blades.
implementation: Aggregate IPS, AV, and AB combined preventions. Prioritize by severity and asset.
cimSpl: | tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Intrusion_Detection.IDS_Attacks by IDS_Attacks.severity | sort - count
knownFalsePositives: Check Point blocks from known pen tests, SandBlast detonations, or temporary policy or identity exceptions; confirm in SmartConsole before treating as a new campaign.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
â€¢ Install and configure the required add-on or app: `Splunk_TA_checkpoint`.
â€¢ Ensure the following data sources are available: `sourcetype=cp:threatprevention`.
â€¢ For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 â€” Configure data collection
Aggregate IPS, AV, and AB combined preventions. Prioritize by severity and asset.

Step 2 â€” Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=security sourcetype="cp:threatprevention" action=prevent
| stats count by protection_type, severity
| sort -count
```

Understanding this SPL

**Check Point Threat Prevention Extraction Blocks** â€” Threat prevention blocks show inline protection value across blades.

Documented **Data sources**: `sourcetype=cp:threatprevention`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): `Splunk_TA_checkpoint`. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feedâ€”rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: security; **sourcetype**: cp:threatprevention. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Detection type** for this use case: TTP â€” interpret thresholds and fields in that context.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

â€¢ Scopes the data: index=security, sourcetype="cp:threatprevention". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
â€¢ `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by protection_type, severity** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
â€¢ Orders rows with `sort` â€” combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Optional CIM / accelerated variant (same use case, normalized fields via Common Information Model):

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Intrusion_Detection.IDS_Attacks by IDS_Attacks.severity | sort - count
```

Understanding this CIM / accelerated SPL

**Check Point Threat Prevention Extraction Blocks** â€” Threat prevention blocks show inline protection value across blades.

Documented **Data sources**: `sourcetype=cp:threatprevention`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): `Splunk_TA_checkpoint`. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feedâ€”rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

This **CIM or accelerated** block uses normalized field names and/or `tstats` over data models. Enable **acceleration** on the referenced models (and correct CIM knowledge objects) or the search may return nothing.

**Detection type** for this use case: TTP â€” interpret thresholds and fields in that context.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

â€¢ Uses `tstats` against accelerated summaries for data model `Intrusion_Detection.IDS_Attacks` â€” enable acceleration for that model.
â€¢ Orders rows with `sort` â€” combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Enable Data Model Acceleration (and metric indexes for `mstats`) for the models or datasets referenced above; otherwise `tstats`/`mstats` may return no results from summaries.


Step 3 â€” Validate
Run the search over a window that includes both normal production traffic and, if you have one, a documented test (vendor sandbox, lab rule hit, or exercise). Compare a few sample events to the vendorâ€™s own console or reports and tune indexes, fields, and thresholds so alerts are actionable before paging.

Step 4 â€” Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Stacked bar (types), Table.
grandmaExplanation: We use Check Point data and alerts to stay ahead of the risks described in "Check Point Threat Prevention Extraction Blocks". We help the team notice trouble early, while a situation is still small and containable.
dataModelAcceleration: Enable Data Model Acceleration for the CIM `Intrusion_Detection.IDS_Attacks` dataset after normalizing this feed to the model; otherwise the `cimSpl` tstats search may return no rows.
